ollama_format.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

parsed_response.response_text: The average initial risk attitude of households exhibits minor fluctuations across all modeled time steps, with values consistently clustered near zero. Most households maintain risk attitudes within the range of -0.2 to 0.2, indicating relatively neutral dispositions toward livestock stocking decisions under climatic uncertainty. Notably, the third column (mean risk attitude of households.3) begins at -0.2 and asymptotically approaches -0.05 by the final time steps, suggesting gradual normalization of risk perception among this subgroup. Conversely, the eleventh household (households.11) shows a consistent increase from 0.18 to 0.19, indicating stable risk aversion despite environmental fluctuations. The fourteenth column (households.14) remains perpetually at 0.045, acting as a steady baseline for comparison. These patterns align with the E-RO agents' fixed behavioral framework under homog-behav-types?, where intrinsic decision-making mechanisms dominate over adaptive learning interactions. The absence of divergent trends reflects both the system's equilibrium state and the spatial diffusion constraints imposed by the disabled social learning parameters. Statistical smoothness in the last fifteen columns (households.15+) further corroborates systemic symmetry in risk attitude propagation, consistent with the homogeneous population structure enforced by the simulation configuration. {
